# NewsForge v3 — Build Instructions for the OpenClaw Team
**From:** FocusPath advisory (Tom) | **Date:** 2026-07-23
**Repo:** `tnowakow/newsforge-backend` (monorepo: `apps/api`, `apps/web`, `packages/shared`, `prisma`)
**Companion file:** `newsforge-v3.diff` (3,235 lines, generated by git, verified to apply cleanly against current `main` @ `41d7a77`)

---

## 0. Why v3 exists (read this first — it frames every task)

Code review of the current demo found three structural problems behind the client-facing weaknesses:

1. **The "AI generation" wasn't designing anything.** Gemini only picked a template ID and wrote filler text; layout placement was a deterministic greedy fitter. The vibrant demo page was **hardcoded** in `renderHtml.ts` — fixed birthday names, fixed event lists, and a `trilogy-rich` branch keyed off the client's name with regex title matching. The layout model had no visual vocabulary (no panel colors, no captions, no list structures), so the AI *could not* produce vibrant pages even in principle.
2. **The "Canva-like" editor was button-nudging** — arrow keys and toolbar buttons moving blocks one coarse cell at a time on a 12×10 grid. No drag, no resize handles.
3. **"InDesign export" was a zip of .txt files** + images + a README asking the designer to rebuild by hand. Not IDML.

v3 fixes the architecture for all three. The diff delivers a working baseline; your tasks below finish, tune, and validate it.

## 1. Applying the diff

```bash
git checkout -b v3 main          # main must be at 41d7a77 or later-compatible
git apply --check newsforge-v3.diff   # verify (should be silent)
git apply --index newsforge-v3.diff
npm ci
npm run prisma:generate
npm run build:shared             # REQUIRED before typechecking/building api or web
npm run typecheck                # api + shared — must be clean
npx tsc -p apps/web/tsconfig.json --noEmit
(cd apps/api && npm test)        # 31 unit tests must pass
npx tsx prisma/seedV3.ts         # idempotent — seeds the 5 v3 spread templates
```

All of the above was executed and verified green during diff authoring **except** `prisma generate` engine download and DB-dependent steps (sandbox had no Prisma engine access or database). Marcus: run the full sequence first and confirm.

## 2. What the diff delivers (per area)

### 2.1 Shared schema — the new visual vocabulary (`packages/shared/schemas/`)
- **`blockStyle.ts` (new):** `PanelToken` (12 tokens: 3 brand-mapped + 9 fixed palette), `BlockStyle` (panel bg, headerColor, invertText, cornerRadius, scriptHeading, centered), `ListItem` (label/value rows with group headers).
- **`run.ts`:** `LayoutBlock` gains `style`, `heading`, `caption`, `listItems`, and a new kind `"list"` (birthdays, event schedules). All optional — v2 layouts still parse.
- **`template.ts`:** grid widened to 24 columns max; slot type `"list"` added; `SlotTypes.list` added.
- **`bundle.ts`:** bundle kind enum accepts `"list"`.
- Colors live in the document as **tokens, never hex** — brand-kit changes restyle every stored layout. Token→hex maps: `apps/api/src/services/designLanguage.ts` (canonical) and `apps/web/src/lib/v3.ts` (mirror). **Riley: add a parity test.**

### 2.2 AI layout generation (`apps/api/src/services/`)
- **`designLanguage.ts` (new):** the house style distilled from the five reference newsletters, as both the palette and `DESIGN_LANGUAGE_PROMPT` (a named export so audit logging can capture the exact spec the model saw).
- **`aiLayoutDesigner.ts` (new):** `designLayout()` — Gemini receives content excerpts, brand kit, the template skeleton (stable blockIds), and the design-language prompt; returns the full styled `AssembledLayout`. 25s timeout (`gemini.ts` now takes per-call `timeoutMs`). Response is Zod-validated, refs sanitized (dead articleId/imageId dropped, duplicate image placements dropped, geometry clamped), forgotten images re-attached.
- **`vibrancyPass.ts` (new):** deterministic normalizer that runs after EITHER path and guarantees the design language: `panel:*` styleTags become panel fills, birthday-ish content converts to sun-panel list blocks (with a `parseListItems` extractor for "Name 7/12" / "7/03 Event" lines), ED-corner content gets the cream panel + script heading, feature headers get rotating colors, every image gets a caption, dark panels invert text. **Output is styled even with no Gemini key.**
- **`routes/runs.ts`:** run creation now calls `designLayout()` instead of bare `assembleLayout()`.

### 2.3 Rendering (`apps/api/src/services/renderHtml.ts` — full rewrite)
100% data-driven print HTML: panels, colored section headings, script headings, list blocks, captioned photos, slim mastheads, brand footer rules. **All hardcoded client branches and fake content are gone.** Handles both 12-col legacy and 24-col v3 grids via `gridSpec`.

### 2.4 Editor (`apps/web/`)
- **`NewsletterRender.tsx` (full rewrite, prop-compatible):** renders the v3 vocabulary AND adds direct manipulation — pointer-drag to move, SE corner handle to resize, converted to grid steps through the *existing* `onMoveBlock`/`onResizeBlock` callbacks, so `Workspace.tsx`/`Preview.tsx` compile untouched. Selection ring, hover ring, floating mini-toolbar (duplicate / layer / delete).
- **`EditableCanvas.tsx` (full rewrite, prop-compatible):** keyboard layer kept (arrows nudge, Alt resizes, Delete removes), 24-col aware clamping, zoom control (60/80/100%).
- **`lib/types.ts` / `lib/v3.ts`:** type mirror + palette/`inferColumns` helpers.

### 2.5 IDML export (`apps/api/src/services/idmlService.ts` + `routes/exportsV3.ts`)
`POST /api/runs/:id/export/idml` → outer zip: `newsletter.idml` + `Links/` (local uploads embedded; remote mock images listed as relink notes) + README. The IDML contains: 2 letter pages, a TextFrame per text/list block at exact grid geometry with panel fills as document swatches, `NF Heading/Body/Caption/ListRow` paragraph styles on the brand fonts, one Story per frame, Rectangle+Image+Link per photo. Complements (does not replace) the existing raw `indesign-bundle`.

### 2.6 Templates (`prisma/seedV3.ts` — standalone, idempotent)
Five 2-page **inner-spread** templates (24×16 grid) recreated from the five reference PDFs: `v3-spread-classic`, `v3-panel-garden`, `v3-resident-feature`, `v3-photo-festival`, `v3-editorial-light` — spanning sparse→photo-dominant, with birthdays/schedule/events list slots, ED-corner cream slots, collage groups, and caption-bearing image slots. Richness hints make them candidates for all non-Trilogy clients.

## 3. Your tasks (the diff intentionally stops here)

### Marcus (backend)
- **M1.** Run §1 end-to-end against a real DB; fix anything environment-specific. Commit the diff as its own commit before your changes.
- **M2.** Extend `POST /:id/edit` ops so the editor can persist the v3 fields: `set-style` (token-validated), `set-caption`, `set-heading`, `edit-list-items`, `set-image-focal`. The schema already stores them; the op layer must accept them.
- **M3.** Persist `designed.mode` / `designNotes` / `fallbackReason` from `designLayout()` onto the run (new columns or into `layoutFitReport`) so the UI can show "AI-designed" vs "deterministic fallback".
- **M4.** Backlog flag (John to schedule): `candidateTemplatesForClient` still hardcodes `client.name === "Trilogy Health Services"` — replace with a data-driven flag on Client. Same smell we just removed from the renderer.

### Sofia + Maya (frontend/UX)
- **F1.** Wire M2 ops into the editor: a right-side inspector for the selected block — panel token swatch picker (from `lib/v3.ts` palette), header-color picker, caption/heading text inputs, list-item editor (add/remove/reorder rows, group headers).
- **F2.** **Inline text editing:** double-click a text block → contentEditable in place → persist via the article-update path. This is the biggest remaining gap to "Canva-class"; design for it, don't bolt it on.
- **F3.** Image tools in the new chrome: verify the pre-v3 focal/zoom controls still reach `NewsletterRender` (it honors `focalX/focalY/zoom`); add drag-inside-frame to set focal point. Acceptance: the "his feet are cut off — raise the image within its frame" scenario, mouse-only.
- **F4.** Verify undo history (commit `c2483e1`) still captures v3 field changes — it hooks `onLayoutChange`, which we preserved, but confirm and add coverage.
- **F5.** Surface `designNotes` + generation mode badge (from M3) in the Workspace, and an "Export IDML" button calling the new endpoint.

### Riley (QA)
- **Q1.** **IDML validation is the critical path.** The generated IDML is spec-derived but was NOT opened in real InDesign during authoring (no InDesign in the build environment). Golden-file test: generate for each of the 5 templates with mock content, open in InDesign, screenshot vs. web render. Expect 1–2 iteration rounds on the XML (likely suspects: PathPointArray winding, ItemTransform on Image, font substitution behavior). File issues per discrepancy; Marcus fixes.
- **Q2.** Unit tests for `vibrancyPass` (golden per template: birthday→sun list, ED→cream, header rotation, caption guarantee) and `parseListItems` (name/date and date/event corpora, group headers, garbage lines).
- **Q3.** Palette parity test: `designLanguage.FIXED_PALETTE` === `web/lib/v3.FIXED_PALETTE`.
- **Q4.** Editor interaction tests: drag moves by grid steps, resize clamps at 24 cols, keyboard still works, prop-compat with Workspace/Preview (no console errors on all pages).
- **Q5.** Regression: 12-col legacy templates still render and edit (inferColumns fallback path).

### Vitaly (architect) — prompt/quality ownership with Bob
- **V1.** Tune `DESIGN_LANGUAGE_PROMPT` against all 5 templates × sparse/moderate/dense mock content until AI output visually matches the reference PDFs' character (panel variety, header color variety, no white space, captions everywhere). Budget several iterations; log before/after per change. Success bar: a fresh run on `v3-spread-classic` with RICH mock content is recognizably "the house style" without manual edits.
- **V2.** Decide retry posture for design calls: current = 2 retries @ 25s then deterministic fallback. Consider a "regenerate with AI" action that surfaces the fallback reason instead of silently falling back on create.

### Sam (security)
- **S1.** Review `exportsV3` route: no auth beyond run-id (consistent with existing export route, but confirm acceptable for demo); export files land in the public `/pdfs/` static dir — same exposure model as PDFs, sign off or gate.
- **S2.** `idmlService` reads upload-dir files by path derived from stored image URLs — verify no traversal (paths come from our own upload service, but assert `path.resolve` containment anyway).

### Ron (DevOps)
- **R1.** Railway release command: append `&& npx tsx prisma/seedV3.ts` after the existing seed.
- **R2.** Confirm `GEMINI_API_KEY` set in the deployed env (without it, everything works but generation is deterministic-fallback styled — fine for uptime, wrong for demoing "AI").

### Dana (docs)
- **D1.** Update README: the repo layout section is stale vs. reality (predates `apps/web`); document the v3 endpoints, seedV3 step, and the design-language/token concept.

### John (PM) / Bob (orchestrator)
- Sequence: **M1 → (Q1 ‖ V1 ‖ F1–F3) → F4/F5, Q2–Q5 → S, R, D.** Q1 and V1 are the two genuinely uncertain workstreams — start both immediately; everything else is deterministic execution.
- One step per session per the Sequential Task Runner pattern; each task above is sized to be one runner step except V1 and Q1 (split per template).

## 4. Acceptance criteria for v3 (demo definition of done)

1. Creating a run for a non-Trilogy client with RICH mock content produces, with zero manual edits, a 2-page spread with: ≥4 distinct panel colors, a birthday list block on the sun panel, a cream ED-corner, colored varied section headers, and a caption under every photo — visually in the family of the 5 reference PDFs.
2. A designer can, mouse-only: drag any block to a new position, resize via the corner handle, recolor a panel, edit a caption, fix a birthday list entry, and replace/reframe a photo (F1–F3).
3. `POST /:id/export/idml` returns a package that opens in InDesign with all frames/text/styles placed and images linked (post-Q1 sign-off).
4. PDF preview, print PDF, editor canvas, and IDML all derive from the same document model — change a panel color once, see it in all four.
5. No client-name conditionals anywhere in the render path (`git grep -i trilogy apps/api/src/services/renderHtml.ts` returns nothing — it already does; keep it that way).

## 5. Known caveats (stated so nobody rediscovers them the hard way)

- **IDML is unvalidated in real InDesign** (see Q1). Treat it as "basic IDML, expect iteration," not "done."
- The two `runs.ts` implicit-any notes some tooling may show at lines ~87/99 only occur when Prisma's generated types are absent; with `prisma generate` they don't exist.
- `designLayout` runs the skeleton fitter twice (skeleton + fallback) — cheap and deliberate for safety; optimize only if profiling says so.
- Frontend infers grid width from block extents (12 vs 24). If a future template legitimately uses ≤12 cols of a 24-col grid on every block, inference guesses low — acceptable now; the durable fix is passing `gridSpec` to the SPA (backlog).
- Mock images are remote URLs → IDML lists them as relink notes rather than embedding. Real uploads embed. Fine for demo; flag in client conversations.
- Demo scope only: no HIPAA controls here by explicit decision — the production posture lives in the separate v1 requirements/architecture package (docs 01–04).
